item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
FOR THE LOVE OF GOD LET THE BENEVOLENT MAGICAL SCARECROW MAN DO HIS FUCKING JOB YOU IDIOTS

sorry, just had to get that off my chest

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is my favorite.
By the way,who is Kawamoto Daisuke based on?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess this means Kakashi would likely be classified as Hiemal, given his absence has caused a spike in supernatural-based maladies and calamities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus was a "benign humanoid with magical capabilities" and tons of bad shit came out of letting the world know about his existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something tells me that the Foundation screwed up seriously with this one. Since this entity is rather benign and does not seem to be any trouble, I don't see any reason why releasing it would be any issue. In fact if doing this would stop the problems then that's all of the proof you really need.

A more effective thing to do would be to make the entire village the containment site and just maintain a passive observation. Since this is already an "open" secret then why not an "open" containment site. You could easily justify the internet video as being a cleverly staged fake. If this SCP can and has been keeping something more dangerous contained then the Foundation has the duty to allow it to return.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(disclaimer: not Japanese, anyone with real qualifications feel free to correct this)

I would agree with you if the scarecrow had appeared in some random country, but this is a spirit in Japan talking about established concepts from dominant Japanese spirituality/folklore/culture. Apart from many Japanese people already believing in yōkai anyway, the others are now faced with a spirit claiming that other spirits are also real - which their grandparents believe in, more spiritual people also believe in, they have grown up hearing stories about and continue to be exposed to various beliefs around spirits. In this situation accepting the folk traditions as a whole package makes sense to explain this supernatural occurrence.

It's a bit like a biblical angel appearing in a Western country, and everyone, even atheists, agreeing 'yep this is definitely an angel', but refusing to accept the angel's pronouncements about demons or Christianity. I would have a hard time justifying that even as a non-religious person myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your bottom quote is fairly accurate, and I see what you're talking about but I disagree with the top point. I would believe the villagers are simply employing the classic phrase "seeing is believing" in their dismissal of the yōkai. It wouldn't be an incredible notion to assume that seeing a weird possessed scarecrow punch at nothing, would cause people to believe that he is, in fact, punching at nothing. I don't doubt that some people would believe it but for the sake of simplicity, it is safe to assume that most wouldn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cada, you are an absolute powerhouse at creating and fleshing out the most believable characters. You're on fire with these.

E- this was meant to be its own comment oops: s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The majority of the population, however, saw no evidence of SCP-4449's claims of combating yōkai and merely assumed that SCP-4449 was some form of delusional ghost.

I have some difficulty imagining people who are prepared to acknowledge the existence of a ghost but dismiss its claims of evil spirits with a requirement for hard evidence.

As of late 2017, Higashishirakawa's infectious disease, genetic disorder, schizophrenia, major depressive disorder, suicide, cancer, and infant mortality diagnosis rates have risen to more than quintuple the national per capita average, to the point of exceeding total rates reported by towns with ten times Higashishirakawa's population.

I doubt whether the baseline absolute numbers of infant mortality or genetic disorder in a <2000 population Japanese rural town are high enough such that a fivefold or even tenfold increase would be noticeable as statistically significant.

This is played very straight as a helpful spirit with a moral lesson about how removing its influence causes bad stuff to happen. This shows up so many times in media everywhere and also on the SCP wiki that I can't get excited about it. It reminds me of SCP-2788, but with less social/historical commentary. This was nicely executed enough that I novoted, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So was the scarecrow the samurai atoning for his sins? :p.

I liked this a lot, +1. It's impressive how well you built the character and the world up without interviews or any kind of log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Volunteer firefighting, during which SCP-4449 would charge into burning buildings to rescue trapped or injured individuals, despite its existence as a flammable scarecrow

/slams that upvote button

Man, I never realized how much I liked bullet point lists with quirky information until I read this. It's something that I've seen used for character development (it feels more professional and immersive for me!) and I've done myself, but man. That was a fun bullet point list to read.

Also, not going to lie, my interest was piqued because I saw the author post and Hatake Kakashi from Naruto is one of my favorite characters.

item.title: domo arigato gozaimashita, kakashi-san
item.content:encoded:
a legendary warrior with a dark past and also straw stuffing.

many thanks to everyone who helped me brush this up.

and arigato to you, reader, for being there. ♥

recommended listening
jam out samurai style

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Df61VVDVsFg
"mirage", by the yoshida brothers ♥
